successCriteria.description: src/shared/types/index.ts exists and exports EmploymentStatus enum with exactly four members: Active, Inactive, Terminated, OnLeave
src/shared/types/index.ts exports LeaveStatus enum with exactly four members: Pending, Approved, Rejected, Cancelled
src/shared/types/index.ts exports BaseEntity interface with fields: id (string), createdAt (Date), updatedAt (Date), deletedAt (Date | null)
src/shared/base.repository.ts exists and exports an abstract class BaseRepository<T> that imports and uses the pg Pool from src/shared/db/connection.ts

BaseRepository<T> provides at least these five CRUD methods: findById(id: string): Promise<T | null>, findAll(): Promise<T[]>, create(entity: Omit<T, 'id' | 'createdAt' | 'updatedAt'>): Promise<T>, update(id: string, partial: Partial<T>): Promise<T | null>, softDelete(id: string): Promise<boolean>
Jest unit tests exist in tests/unit/shared/ covering the exported types (enum values, interface shape) and the BaseRepository CRUD methods (mocking the pg Pool)

constraints: All database access must go through the repository pattern — BaseRepository uses the pg Pool from src/shared/db/connection.ts; no inline SQL outside the repository layer
Strict TypeScript: no implicit any, strict null checks enabled (tsconfig strict: true). Use unknown with type guards instead of any
Tests use Jest with ts-jest preset, test files in tests/unit/shared/ matching **/tests/**/*.test.(ts|js)
No hardcoded secrets, passwords, API keys, or tokens anywhere in the codebase
Module structure: shared utilities under src/shared/ — types in src/shared/types/index.ts, base repository in src/shared/base.repository.ts
outOfScope: Phase 2: Employee module — model (Employee interface, CreateEmployeeDto, UpdateEmployeeDto) and repository (IEmployeeRepository extending BaseRepository)
Phase 3: LeaveType and LeavePolicy modules — models, repositories, and barrel files
Phase 4: LeaveBalance module — model, repository, and barrel file
Phase 5: LeaveRequest module — model, repository, and barrel file
Phase 6-10: All service layers, controllers, routes, RBAC middleware, audit logging, notification sending, and balance accrual logic
Database migrations, table creation, or Knex schema definitions — this phase only creates shared TypeScript types and the abstract repository class

ambiguities.description: How does the abstract BaseRepository know which database table to operate on? The intent does not specify whether subclasses provide the table name via a constructor parameter, an abstract property getter, or a protected field.
options: Abstract property: protected abstract tableName: string
Protected field set by subclass: protected tableName: string

ambiguities.description: Should findAll() exclude soft-deleted records (deletedAt IS NOT NULL) by default? The intent does not specify whether the base repository filters out soft-deleted rows.
options: Yes, findAll excludes soft-deleted rows by default (WHERE deleted_at IS NULL)
No, findAll returns all rows including soft-deleted; filtering is the caller's responsibility
Provide an optional parameter: findAll(includeDeleted?: boolean)

ambiguities.description: The create method signature in the intent omits 'id', 'createdAt', and 'updatedAt' from the input type, but BaseEntity also has 'deletedAt'. Should 'deletedAt' also be omitted from the create input (since it defaults to null on creation)?
options: Yes, omit deletedAt from create input — it always defaults to null
No, allow callers to pass deletedAt in create

ambiguities.description: AGENTS.md states the test framework is Vitest, but HARNESS.json, package.json, jest.config.js, and the intent text all specify Jest. Which test framework should be used?
options: Jest (matches HARNESS.json, package.json, jest.config.js, and the intent)
